title: chrysanthemum maritimum var. inodorum
intro: Chrysanthemum maritimum var. inodorum, also known as Sea Chrysanthemum or Sea Daisy, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally used in Eastern medicine, Sea Chrysanthemum has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Sea Chrysanthemum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Sea Chrysanthemum are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
